name: Iolani Palace
tagline: The only royal palace on American soil — home to Hawaii's last monarchs, with electricity before the White House.
parking: Metered street parking; paid lots on King St
bestTime: Morning guided tours; Friday noon concerts by the Royal Hawaiian Band on the grounds (free)
talkStory: Completed in 1882 for King Kalakaua, Iolani had electric lights four years before the White House and telephones before Buckingham Palace. After the 1893 overthrow, Queen Liliuokalani was imprisoned upstairs for eight months — you can see the quilt she sewed during confinement. The palace fell into severe disrepair and a massive restoration beginning in 1969 recovered original furnishings from basements across the state. The koa wood staircase alone took artisans years to restore.
